name: Citrus-Buttered Lobster Tails
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the frozen lobster tails in a shallow baking dish.
2. Micro-cook, covered, on 30% power for 7 to 8 minutes or until thawed, rotating the dish a quarter-turn once. The tails are thawed when the shells are flexible enough to bend.
3. Using a heavy knife, cut through the center of the top shells. Continue cutting through the meat but not through the undershells.
4. Spread the tails open, butterfly style, so the meat is on top. Return them to the shallow baking dish.
5. Pour water atop the tails and micro-cook, covered, on 50% power for 6 to 8 minutes or until the meat is opaque, rotating the dish a quarter-turn every minute. (Shield cooked meat with small pieces of foil if necessary, to prevent overcooking.)
6. Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes.
7. Meanwhile, combine butter or margarine, lemon juice, orange peel, salt, ginger, and paprika. Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power for 1½ to 2 minutes or until the butter is melted. Mix well.
8. Drizzle the lobster tails with the butter mixture.
